# validate-services-data

The validate-services-data action runs schema checks on the provided list of OBS Studio service configuration files before optionally running proprietary checks on the services contained in the file.

The infrastructure to run these service checks is not public and requires access information provided to the action.

## Documentation

### Inputs

| Input | Description | Default |
|:-----:|-------------|---------|
| `path` | A string of either a JSON array of files, a glob expression, or a relative file path. | `REQUIRED` |
| `api-token` | An API token for the server check service. | `REQUIRED` |
| `api-servers` | A comma-separated list of server check service endpoints. | `REQUIRED` |
| `run-schema-checks` | A boolean value to indicate whether the JSON schema of provided files should be checked. | `true` |
| `run-service-checks` | A boolean value to indicate whether the server check service should be used to check availability of the services. | `false` |
| `purge-age` | The grace period in seconds after which an unresponsive service is considered for removal. | `842400` |
| `create-pull-request` | A boolean value to indicate whether a pull request with a list of all services considered for removal should be created by the action. | `false` |
| `github-token`| The GitHub token required to optionally create a pull request. The provided token needs to have the `pull-requests: write` permission. | `github.token`|
| `working-directory` | A path to the root directory of an OBS Studio checkout. | `github.workspace` |

### Outputs

| Output | Description |
|:------:|-------------|
| `has-defunct-services` | A boolean string to indicate whether any defunct services were detected. |

## Common Usage

The action is designed to handle the output of the `check-changes` action, so that they can be used in combination with each other, but can also optionally handle glob expressions directly.

```yaml
      - name: Check Services
        uses: ./.github/actions/lint-obs/validate-services-data
        with:
          path: 'services/*.json'
          api-token: ${{ secrets.server-check-api-key }}
          api-servers: ${{ secrets.server-check-api-servers }}
          run-schema-checks: true
          run-service-checks: true
          create-pull-request: true
          purge-age: 168000
```

## Notes

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The action requires a macOS or Linux GitHub Actions runner.

* The server check service is a proprietary service used by the OBS Project and not available to the public.

> [!WARNING]
> Secrets should always be stored as environment secrets (and not repository secrets) as this allows a project to require approval by organization members before an associated workflow actually executes and accesses these secrets.


## Developer Notes

The action uses a custom Python script to communicate with the server check service and internally uses GraphQL to identify authors behind specific URLs in the services JSON file.

The Python script requires the https://github.com/psf/requests and https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp Python modules.

As the pull request body generated by the action will ping those authors, running this action too often with too low a `purge-age` has the potential to be considered "spamming" and should thus be used with care.
